mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is an AI Gateway that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features like cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it suitable for applications that require interaction with multiple language models. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and potential costs associated with API usage.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the LiteLLM command-line interface to manage your deployments.

```bash

pip install litellm==1.82.dev2

```

> Install the specific version of LiteLLM.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new Kubernetes cluster using gcloud with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Adjust region and node count as needed.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure kubectl to use the new cluster's context.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command sets the kubeconfig to point to the new cluster.

steps.title: Verify Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in the cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> This command applies the deployment configuration.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for LiteLLM.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl create rolebinding litellm-admin --clusterrole=edit --serviceaccount=default:litellm --namespace=default

```

> This command enables autoscaling based on CPU usage.
> This command creates a role binding for RBAC.

uninstall.title: Uninstall LiteLLM
uninstall.description:
Remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss and orphaned resources.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the deployment.
> This command deletes the entire cluster.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM
upgrade.description:
Backup your current deployment, upgrade to the new version, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get all > litellm-backup.yaml

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl rollout status deployment/litellm

```

> Backup current resources.
> Upgrade to the new version.
> Verify that the deployment is updated successfully.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources allocated for the deployment.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or number of nodes in your cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The request to the API may be timing out due to network issues.

**Fix:**
Check your network configuration and ensure the API is reachable.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unauthorized'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** API keys or permissions may not be set correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify that the correct API keys are set in the environment variables.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods' showing the LiteLLM pod running with a status of 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
